title: gastrodia cunninghamii
intro: Gastrodia cunninghamii, also known as the Chinese tiang qi or Black Bat Lily, is a type of orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as gastrodine and gastrodin. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Gastrodia cunninghamii is often combined with other plants to create tonics and decoctions that are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, headache, and rheumatism.
